name: LinearDimension
summary: References a linear dimension entity. A LinearDimension is composed of the following sub-entities: two 'extension lines' that connect to the Entity being dimensioned. a 'dimension line' connecting the ends of the leaders. This may be represented by one or two Paths depending on appearance. an optional 'leader line' that is used for small LinearDimensions. a 'dimension text' that displays the LinearDimension's text. There are six points that may be modified for a LinearDimension: two 'connection points' that define the start and end of the LinearDimension. two 'extent points' that define the start and end of the dimension line and are the ends of the two extension lines. two 'offset points' that define the starting points of the extension lines.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new disconnected Layout::LinearDimension.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new disconnected Layout::LinearDimension.

params.name: height
params.doc: Distance from the start and end points to the dimension line

throws.when: if alignment is not a valid alignment type
if a dimension couldn't be created with the provided arguments

methods.name: auto_scale?
methods.summary: The #auto_scale? method returns whether the scale for the Layout::LinearDimension is set automatically.
methods.remarks: The #auto_scale? method returns whether the scale for the Layout::LinearDimension is set automatically.

methods.name: connect
methods.summary: The #connect method connects the Layout::LinearDimension to one or two Entitys using the provided ConnectionPoints.
methods.remarks: The #connect method connects the Layout::LinearDimension to one or two Entitys using the provided ConnectionPoints. The Layout::LinearDimension must be in the same Document as the Entitys, and on the same Page if the Entitys are on non-shared Layout::Layers.

throws.when: if the Layout::LinearDimension is on a locked Layout::Layer

methods.name: custom_text?
methods.summary: The #custom_text? method returns whether the Layout::LinearDimension uses custom text.
methods.remarks: The #custom_text? method returns whether the Layout::LinearDimension uses custom text. When true, the Layout::LinearDimension will display a custom string that doesn't change. When false, it will display the length measurement and will update automatically.

methods.name: disconnect
methods.summary: The #disconnect method disconnects the Layout::LinearDimension from its ConnectionPoints.
methods.remarks: The #disconnect method disconnects the Layout::LinearDimension from its ConnectionPoints. The dimension will not be adjusted by disconnecting from its ConnectionPoints.

throws.when: if the Layout::LinearDimension is on a locked Layout::Layer

methods.name: end_offset_point
methods.summary: The #end_offset_point method returns the paper space location for the end of the first extension line.
methods.remarks: The #end_offset_point method returns the paper space location for the end of the first extension line. The first extension line runs from this offset point to the end extent point.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The #entities method returns the Entities that represent the Layout::LinearDimension in its exploded form.
methods.remarks: The #entities method returns the Entities that represent the Layout::LinearDimension in its exploded form. Depending on the appearance of the Layout::LinearDimension, this may return anywhere from four to six Entitys: start extension line, end extension line, one or two dimension lines, dimension text, and optionally the leader line.

methods.name: leader_line_visible?
methods.summary: The #leader_line_visible? method returns whether the leader line is currently visible.
methods.remarks: The #leader_line_visible? method returns whether the leader line is currently visible.

methods.name: start_offset_point
methods.summary: The #start_offset_point method returns the paper space location for the start of the first extension line.
methods.remarks: The #start_offset_point method returns the paper space location for the start of the first extension line. The first extension line runs from this offset point to the start extent point.

properties.name: auto_scale
properties.summary: The #auto_scale= method sets whether the scale for the Layout::LinearDimension is set automatically.
properties.remarks: The #auto_scale= method sets whether the scale for the Layout::LinearDimension is set automatically.

properties.name: custom_text
properties.summary: The #custom_text= method sets whether the Layout::LinearDimension uses custom text.
properties.remarks: The #custom_text= method sets whether the Layout::LinearDimension uses custom text. When true, the Layout::LinearDimension will display a custom string that doesn't change. When false, it will display the length measurement and will update automatically.

properties.name: end_connection_point
properties.summary: The #end_connection_point method returns the paper space location for the second connection.
properties.remarks: The #end_connection_point method returns the paper space location for the second connection.

properties.name: end_extent_point
properties.summary: The #end_extent_point method returns the paper space location for the end of the dimension line.
properties.remarks: The #end_extent_point method returns the paper space location for the end of the dimension line.

properties.name: end_offset_length
properties.summary: The #end_offset_length= method sets the length of the offset from the second ConnectionPoint to the start of the second extension line.
properties.remarks: The #end_offset_length= method sets the length of the offset from the second ConnectionPoint to the start of the second extension line. The ConnectionPoint and extent point will not move.

properties.name: leader_line_type
properties.summary: The #leader_line_type method returns the type of leader line the Layout::LinearDimension is using.
properties.remarks: The #leader_line_type method returns the type of leader line the Layout::LinearDimension is using. The leader line type can be one of the following: Layout::LinearDimension::LEADER_LINE_TYPE_SINGLE_SEGMENT Layout::LinearDimension::LEADER_LINE_TYPE_TWO_SEGMENT Layout::LinearDimension::LEADER_LINE_TYPE_BEZIER Layout::LinearDimension::LEADER_LINE_TYPE_HIDDEN

properties.name: scale
properties.summary: The #scale method returns the scale being used for the Layout::LinearDimension.
properties.remarks: The #scale method returns the scale being used for the Layout::LinearDimension.

properties.name: start_connection_point
properties.summary: The #start_connection_point method returns the paper space location for the first connection.
properties.remarks: The #start_connection_point method returns the paper space location for the first connection.

properties.name: start_extent_point
properties.summary: The #start_extent_point method returns the paper space location for the start of the dimension line.
properties.remarks: The #start_extent_point method returns the paper space location for the start of the dimension line.

properties.name: start_offset_length
properties.summary: The #start_offset_length= method sets the length of the offset from the first ConnectionPoint to the start of the first extension line.
properties.remarks: The #start_offset_length= method sets the length of the offset from the first ConnectionPoint to the start of the first extension line. The ConnectionPoint and extent point will not move.

properties.name: text
properties.summary: Note: With the addition of auto-text in dimensions for LayOut 2019.
properties.remarks: Note: With the addition of auto-text in dimensions for LayOut 2019.2, the copy of the dimension text incorrectly provided the plain text when requesting the display text. This has been fixed for LayOut 2020.1. The #text method returns a copy of the Layout::LinearDimension's FormattedText.
